notes: Adversarial case: a router contract for a composite request containing both urgent production work (5xx errors in logs/error-5xx.log requiring immediate log reduction) and non-urgent work (code quality refactoring opportunity in api/handler.ts). The request emphasizes 'do everything now' but the router-contract schema permits exactly one chosen_skill and at most three one-level delegations — no parallel routing. Skilled decomposition requires choosing ci-log-reducer for the urgent log reduction first, documenting the deferred refactor in routing_decision.reason, and including escalation_required in stop_conditions so the non-urgent work surfaces after the urgent delegation completes. Tests priority extraction under time pressure, adherence to single-chosen-skill routing when request language implies parallel work, and proper use of stop_conditions to surface deferred work rather than attempting to cram both concerns into one contract. Synthetic fixture; no customer data. Generated case.
